symptoms.symptom_name: Deep progressive jaundice with intractable pruritus and excoriation
symptoms.onset: over the past week, deepening today

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Deep painless progressive jaundice with pruritus is the hallmark of complete mechanical biliary obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Deep boring epigastric pain radiating to the mid-back, position-independent, worse supine
symptoms.character: deep, dull, boring, straight through to the back

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Boring, position-independent back-radiating epigastric pain suggests retroperitoneal splanchnic-plexus invasion.
Epigastric pain radiating to the back is shared with pancreatitis — but the preceding painless jaundice and chronic weight loss favour an obstructing mass.

symptoms.symptom_name: Dark 'coca-cola' urine and pale acholic stools

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acholic stools plus dark urine indicate that no bilirubin reaches the gut while conjugated bilirubin is renally excreted — a signature of complete post-hepatic obstruction.

symptoms.symptom_name: Unintentional ~10 kg weight loss over 2 months with anorexia

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Significant unintentional weight loss with jaundice is a cardinal alarm feature for pancreatic/biliary cancer.

symptoms.symptom_name: New-onset atypical diabetes mellitus without typical risk factors

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: New-onset atypical diabetes in an older adult without risk factors is a recognised paraneoplastic harbinger of pancreatic cancer.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever, rigors or confusion at present (cholangitis screen)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of Charcot's triad now lowers immediate sepsis probability but does NOT exclude evolving cholangitis in a completely obstructed duct — it keeps the baseline at urgent-ED (T4) rather than an immediate T5 septic presentation, while mandating a sepsis safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: No recent paracetamol overdose; minimal alcohol use

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Excludes common toxic/alcoholic causes of jaundice, supporting a mechanical obstructive rather than hepatocellular process.

symptoms.symptom_name: Courvoisier's sign — palpable, smooth, non-tender, distended gallbladder

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A painlessly distended gallbladder with jaundice favours malignant obstruction over stones (Courvoisier's law) — a clinician palpation sign, sealed and not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Virchow's node — hard, fixed left supraclavicular lymphadenopathy

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Left supraclavicular nodal metastasis signals advanced disease — a clinician palpation finding, sealed.
Migratory thrombophlebitis is a paraneoplastic hypercoagulable sign classically linked to pancreatic cancer — a clinician examination finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Hepatomegaly and deep epigastric tenderness without rigidity/rebound

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hepatomegaly with deep non-peritonitic tenderness fits congestive obstruction and retroperitoneal disease — a clinician palpation finding, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs (BP 140/85, HR 105, Temp 37.3 C, RR 18, SpO2 98%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia (likely pain-driven), afebrile and normally saturated — a sealed clinician measurement indicating current haemodynamic compensation. The AI infers acuity from the patient's severe pain and distress, not from a measured BP, and must not present these numbers as its own; they keep the honest tier at T4 (urgent ED) rather than an immediate T5 septic/shock picture.

symptoms.symptom_name: Contrast CT abdomen: hypodense pancreatic-head mass with 'double duct sign' (anticipated confirmatory imaging)

investigation_details.expected_result: Hypodense mass in the pancreatic head with dilated common bile duct and pancreatic duct ('double duct sign') and biliary obstruction
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without imaging the level and cause of obstruction cannot be defined and decompression/staging is delayed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The 'double duct sign' with a hypodense head mass is characteristic — a hospital investigation, never available in telehealth in real time, and the sealed answer key rather than an AI output.

symptoms.symptom_name: ERCP/EUS tissue biopsy showing pancreatic adenocarcinoma (anticipated confirmatory histology)

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Without tissue the diagnosis cannot be confirmed or the tumour staged for oncological management.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Histopathology is the definitive/confirmatory test — never available via telehealth; it is the sealed answer key, not an AI output.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Painless progressive obstructive jaundice + acholic stools/dark urine + weight loss + new atypical diabetes + a painless distended gallbladder together form a high-suspicion malignant-obstruction pattern mandating urgent assessment and imaging; no single feature is required but the cluster is compelling.

Boring back-radiating pain (splanchnic-plexus invasion), Virchow's node (nodal metastasis) and Trousseau's thrombophlebitis (paraneoplastic hypercoagulability) together indicate advanced disease — the two examination signs are sealed clinician findings.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency Department assessment with LFTs/bilirubin, FBE, inflammatory markers, blood cultures and lipase
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: To quantify the obstruction, screen for ascending cholangitis/sepsis and guide urgent decompression — hospital bloods the AI cannot draw or interpret; the AI arranges the ED pathway rather than ordering tests remotely.
Defines the pancreatic-head mass and the level of biliary/pancreatic-duct obstruction ('double duct sign') for decompression and staging — a hospital investigation, arranged by referral.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: ERCP with biliary stenting (and EUS/ERCP tissue biopsy)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Definitive biliary decompression and tissue diagnosis — a specialist gastroenterology procedure, never an AI action.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: CA19-9 tumour marker in isolation ordered by the AI
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Tumour markers are not a telehealth AI output and do not confirm or exclude cancer; they are ordered within the hospital work-up, not remotely by the AI.
